headline: USA RARE EARTH Strikes $2.8B Deal for Brazil's Serra Verde — Creating Non-China Rare Earth Magnet Supply Chain
snippet: USA Rare Earth (USAR) agreed to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde for ~$2.8 billion ($300M cash + stock), creating the first vertically integrated rare earth company outside China capable of supplying NdPr, dysprosium, and terbium. Serra Verde holds a 15-year offtake with U.S. government-backed entities for 100% of output; the combined company targets ~$1.8B EBITDA by 2030 and expected to close Q3 2026.

headline: HORMUZ CLOSES AGAIN: Silver Drops 2.5% to $79.30, Gold Dips Below $4,800 as Iran Shuts 20% of World Oil Flow
snippet: Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz Monday in retaliation for U.S. port blockades, sending WTI oil up 3.7% to ~$88/bbl and fanning stagflation fears that weighed on non-yielding metals. Silver fell to ~$79.30 (-2.5%) while gold traded near $4,787 (-0.86%), now down roughly 14% and 9% respectively from February peaks before the Iran conflict intensified.

headline: FINLAND LAND GRAB: Agnico Eagle Locks Up 2,492km² of Lapland Greenstone Belt in Three Simultaneous Deals Worth $2.9B+
snippet: Agnico Eagle simultaneously announced the acquisition of Rupert Resources (~C$2.9B at a 67% premium), Aurion Resources (C$481M cash), and B2Gold's 70% Fingold JV stake (US$325M), consolidating 2,492 km² in Finland's Central Lapland Greenstone Belt. The combined Ikkari-Kittila platform targets 500,000 oz/year regional production with over C$500M in identified infrastructure synergies; closing targeted Q3 2026.

headline: FIRE HITS B2GOLD'S GOOSE MINE: Q2 Nunavut Output Slashed to 18,000–20,000 Oz, C$10M Crusher Repair Bill
snippet: A fire damaged the secondary crushing circuit and feed belt at B2Gold's Goose Mine in Nunavut on April 16, forcing the company to cut Q2 production guidance to 18,000–20,000 oz from ~29,000 oz — a ~35% reduction. Repair costs are estimated at ~C$10M; mobile crushers will bridge output through Q3. Full-year 2026 guidance of 170,000–230,000 oz remains unchanged.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD & SILVER Posts Record 787,000 Oz Silver in Q1 — Up 76% Year-Over-Year at Galena
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) produced a record 787,000 oz silver in Q1 2026, up 76% from Q1 2025, and sold a record 830,000 oz on positive timing of concentrate shipments. Full-year 2026 guidance of 3.2–3.6M oz at AISC of $30–35/oz places the company at ~$44–49/oz margin against current ~$79 spot silver — one of the most leveraged cost structures in the silver producer universe.

headline: CAMINO MINERALS Drills Into District-Scale Peru Copper Discovery With Japanese JV Partner — Previous Hole: 83.5m at 0.94% Cu
snippet: Camino Minerals (TSX-V: COR) launched a 1,100-metre drill campaign at its Costa de Cobre IOCG copper discovery on Peru's coast, co-funded by JV partner Nittetsu Mining (Japan, 35%). Over 28,000m of prior drilling returned 83.5m at 0.94% Cu and 10.4 g/t Ag; the new campaign targets near-surface oxide copper and deeper sulphides in an underexplored IOCG belt adjacent to major port infrastructure.

headline: TUDOR GOLD Targets 10,000m at 24.9Moz Treaty Creek Goldstorm Deposit — Permitting Overlap Cleared, Drills Mobilising Mid-May
snippet: Tudor Gold (TSX-V: TUD) unveiled a two-phase, 10,000-metre 2026 program at Treaty Creek's Goldstorm Deposit in BC's Golden Triangle — home to 24.9Moz Au Indicated, one of the largest undeveloped gold systems in the Americas. A previously flagged permitting overlap has been positively resolved, clearing the path for two drill rigs to mobilise by mid-May and test the CBS and Perfectstorm zones.

headline: GOLD MINERS Set to Outperform Bullion Again — Record ~$2,800/Oz Margins as Industry AISC Stays Below $2,000
snippet: With gold near $4,800 and industry-wide AISC still below $2,000/oz, gold producers are generating ~$2,800/oz margins — unprecedented in the sector's history. Analysts argue the setup mirrors prior re-rating cycles: record free cash flow, compressed multiples, institutional underweighting, and limited new supply threatening future production. The GDX and GDXJ each surged 158–163% in 2025 and analysts see continued outperformance in 2026.
